title: MACHINE AND METHOD FOR PRODUCING ROLL PACKAGES AND PACKAGING
abstract: A wrapping machine for rolls of tissue paper, comprising a travel path of the rolls to be packaged, a station and a feed path of a plastic packaging film toward the travel path, arranged and configured to wrap a portion of said plastic film around a roll or group of rolls to be packaged, wherein an embossing assembly is positioned along said feed path of the plastic film, adapted to emboss the plastic film before it is wrapped around the rolls to be packaged.
claims: Claims 1. A wrapping machine for rolls of tissue paper, comprising a travel path of the rolls to be packaged, a station and a feed path of a plastic packaging film toward the travel path, arranged and configured to wrap a portion of said plastic film around a roll or group of rolls to be packaged, wherein an embossing assembly is positioned along said feed path of the plastic film, adapted to emboss the plastic film before it is wrapped around the rolls to be packaged. 2. The wrapping machine of claim 1, wherein the embossing assembly comprises an embossing roller provided with a plurality of embossing protuber- ances and co-acting with a pressure roller forming an embossing nip between the embossing roller and the pressure roller; wherein the embossing roller and the pressure roller are pressed against each other, thus generating an embossing pressure in the embossing nip, through which the plastic film is fed. 3. The wrapping machine of claim 2, wherein the pressure roller is provided with a yieldable coating, preferably a resiliently yieldable coating. 4. The wrapping machine of claim 1 or 2 or 3, wherein a plastic film cutting device is positioned along the feed path of the plastic film. 5. The wrapping machine of claim 4, wherein the cutting device and the embossing assembly are in phase with each other. 6. The wrapping machine of claim 4, wherein members for phasing the embossing formed by the embossing assembly with the cut performed by the cutting device are arranged along the travel path of the plastic film. 7. The wrapping machine of one or more of the preceding claims, comprising a plastic film sealing station. 8. The wrapping machine of claim 7, wherein the plastic film sealing station comprises plastic film welding means. 9. The wrapping machine of one or more of the preceding claims, wherein along the travel path folding members are arranged, adapted to fold flaps of the plastic film to form the pack of rolls. 10. The wrapping machine of claims 7 and 9 or 8 and 9, wherein the sealing station is configured to cause adhesion of folded plastic film flaps. 11. The wrapping machine of claim 9 or 10, wherein the embossing assembly is configured to emboss portions of the plastic film external to said flaps, leaving the flaps substantially free of embossing. 12. The wrapping machine of one or more of the preceding claims, comprising a phasing system for phasing the embossing of the plastic film generated by the embossing assembly and surface features of the plastic film. 13. The wrapping machine of claim 12, wherein the surface features of the plastic film comprise a print, a perforation or combinations thereof. 14. A method for producing packs of rolls of tissue paper wrapped in a plastic film, comprising the steps of: -unwinding a plastic film from a reel; -embossing the plastic film; -cutting a sheet of plastic film; -wrapping the sheet of embossed plastic film around a group of rolls of tissue paper. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the step of embossing the plastic film comprises the step of feeding the plastic film in an embossing nip formed between an embossing roller provided with a plurality of embossing protuberances and a pressure roller; wherein the embossing roller and the pressure roller are pressed against each other generating an embossing pressure in the embossing nip, thus embossing the plastic film. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the pressure roller has a smooth surface; and wherein the pressure roller is preferably provided with a yieldable coating, preferably a resiliently yieldable coating. 17. The method of one or more of claims 14 to 16, wherein the step of wrapping the sheet of plastic film around the group of rolls comprises the steps of: - inserting the sheet of plastic film in a travel path of the groups of rolls to be packaged; - wrapping the sheet of plastic film around the group of rolls by means of folding members along the travel path. 18. The method of one or more of claims 14 to 17, wherein the step of embossing the plastic film comprises the step of embossing portions of plastic film in phase with a surface feature of the plastic film. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the surface feature of the plastic film comprises a print, a perforation, or combinations thereof. 20. The method of one or more of claims 14 to 19, wherein the steps of embossing the plastic film and cutting the sheet of plastic film are coordinated so as to phase embossing with respect to at least one edge of the sheet of plastic film. 21. The method of one or more of claims 14 to 20, wherein the step of wrapping the sheet of plastic film around the group of rolls comprises the step of folding lateral flaps of the sheet of plastic film on flat front surfaces of the rolls and securing said folded flaps. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the step of embossing the plastic film is performed leaving the folded flaps substantially free of embossing. 23. A pack of rolls of tissue paper comprising at least one roll of tissue paper and a sheet of plastic film wrapped around the roll of tissue paper; wherein the sheet of plastic film has at least one embossed portion. 24. The pack of claim 23, wherein the sheet of plastic film comprises at least one surface feature in phase with the embossed portion. 25. The pack of claim 24, wherein the surface feature comprises a print, a perforation, or a combination thereof. 26. The pack of one or more of claims 23 to 25, wherein the sheet of plastic film comprises lateral flaps folded against flat surfaces of the roll or rolls, said folded flaps being adhered to pack, preferably by welding. 27. The pack of claim 26, wherein the folded lateral flaps are free of embossing. 28. The pack of one or more of claims 23 to 27, comprising a perforation line and/or resealable opening, and wherein the perforation line and/or the resealable opening are preferably in phase with the embossed portion of the plastic film. 29. The pack of one or more of claims 23 to 28, wherein the plastic film comprises one or more of the following feature: a density between about 0.7 and about 1.3 g/m 3 , preferably between about 0.9 and about 0.95 g/m 3 ; a thickness between about 15 micrometers and about 45 micrometers, preferably between about 20 micrometers and about 35 micrometers; and is preferably made of polyethylene.
